subject: English
skill_name: How many syllables does the word have?
question_text: Listen to the word. How many syllables does it have?
audio_note: This question requires audio playback of the word. The word itself is not available in text form.

subject: English
skill_name: Sort by the number of syllables
question_text: Listen to each word. Then, sort by the number of syllables. 1 syllable 2 syllables 3 syllables

subject: English
skill_name: Use key details to determine the main idea
question_text: Look at these details from a paragraph about the same topic: Japan experiences a large number of earthquakes every year. Strong earthquakes regularly hit Turkey. In parts of the United States, small and large earthquakes happen every year. Choose the main, or central, idea that ties all the details together.
options: Turkey and Japan experience earthquakes.
Earthquakes occur in places around the world.
Scientists all over the world study earthquakes.

subject: English
skill_name: Use key details to determine the main idea
question_text: Look at these details from a paragraph about the same topic: Birds live in nests, but squirrels also make nests to stay in. Fish make nests of bubbles, sand or plants. Bees build nests either above or below ground. Choose the main, or central, idea that ties all the details together.
options: Most animals build their nests in the summer.
Nests are homes to more than just birds.
Squirrels create nests in holes found in tree trunks or high up in branches.

subject: English
skill_name: Use key details to determine the main idea
question_text: Look at these details from a paragraph about the same topic: Though most chopsticks are made from wood, some are made of bamboo. Chopsticks can also be made of metal, such as silver or brass. Some chopsticks are made of precious stones. Choose the main, or central, idea that ties all the details together.
options: Chopsticks are sometimes made of ocean coral.
Metal and stones can be used to create chopsticks.
Chopsticks can be made out of more than just wood.

subject: English
skill_name: Use key details to determine the main idea
question_text: Look at these details from a paragraph about the same topic: One study found that students did poorly on a test if they saw the colour red first. Some studies have examined how different colours make people feel. Other studies have tried to figure out whether people are more likely to buy packages of a certain colour. Choose the main, or central, idea that ties all the details together.
options: Some studies are trying to figure out if colours help memory.
There are studies to see how colours make people feel and behave.
Some studies are looking at how the colour red makes people behave.

subject: English
skill_name: Use key details to determine the main idea
question_text: Look at these details from a paragraph about the same topic: Bright and beautiful, marigolds add colour to a garden. The heads of marigolds can be used as medicine. Marigolds can bring helpful bugs to a garden. Choose the main, or central, idea that ties all the details together.
options: Marigold petals are used to colour cloth.
Marigolds are useful flowers.
Roses, tulips and marigolds have different uses.

subject: English
skill_name: Determine the main idea of a passage
question_text: What is the main, or central, idea of the passage?
passage: Small Steps to a Happier Planet Recycling things like paper, plastic and metal helps the Earth by cutting down on rubbish. Yet many people throw away their newspapers, bottles and cans instead of recycling them. The question, then, is how to encourage more people to recycle. One way that works is to put recycling containers in more places. Seeing more recycling bins helps people remember to use them. Another way is to teach people why recycling is important. If we make recycling easier and teach people why it matters, more people will join in.
options: We can get more people to recycle by putting out more bins and teaching people about why recycling matters.
We need to get more people to recycle so that we stop throwing out so much rubbish.

subject: English
skill_name: Determine the main idea of a passage
question_text: What is the main, or central, idea of the passage?
passage: Antarctica: Not What You Think When people think of Antarctica, they think of snow and ice. When they think of deserts, they think of sun, heat and sand. Yet in spite of its cold temperatures, Antarctica is actually a desert. In fact, it is the largest desert on the planet. A desert is a very dry environment that receives less than twenty-five centimetres of precipitation yearly. On average, Antarctica gets less than five centimetres. Most of that precipitation is in the form of snow, but rain or ice can sometimes fall as well.
options: Most precipitation in Antarctica is in the form of snow, though it can also get rain or ice.
Even though Antarctica is cold and has snow, it is a desert.

subject: English
skill_name: Determine the main idea of a passage
question_text: What is the main, or central, idea of the passage?
passage: Earth and Mars Earth is much more welcoming to life than Mars is. Earth's thick atmosphere helps keep the planet warm, with an average temperature of 14°C. In addition, over two-thirds of Earth is covered by water. These conditions make it easy for all kinds of organisms to live here. Mars, on the other hand, has a thin atmosphere and is further from the sun. Therefore, it is much colder. Mars's average temperature is about -51°C! In addition, the surface of the planet is mostly dry and dusty. Without enough water and warmth, most organisms would struggle to survive on Mars.
options: Earth has a thick atmosphere and a warm temperature.
Earth is better able to support life than Mars is.

subject: English
skill_name: Determine the main idea of a passage
question_text: What is the main, or central, idea of the passage?
passage: A Symbol of Freedom Edouard de Laboulaye, a French writer and law professor, admired the freedoms enjoyed in the United States. He supported US president Abraham Lincoln's freeing of slaves during the American Civil War. After the war, in 1865, he suggested that France present a statue to the US. The statue was meant to honour Lincoln and American democracy. At that time, the French people had little say in their government. Laboulaye hoped the statue would encourage the French to demand more freedom at home. Work began on the project in 1875. Ten years later, the statue, known as the Statue of Liberty, finally arrived in New York.
options: Edouard de Laboulaye came up with the idea for the Statue of Liberty after the American Civil War ended.
Edouard de Laboulaye wanted the Statue of Liberty to inspire freedom and democracy in France.

subject: English
skill_name: Determine the main idea of a passage
question_text: What is the main, or central, idea of the passage?
passage: Dogs and Wolves Dogs and wolves are part of the same animal family. Both animals have strong senses of smell and are loyal to their packs. But dogs and wolves are quite different when it comes to people. Dogs are used to living with people and get along well with them. In fact, dogs have been called 'man's best friend'. Wolves, on the other hand, cannot live with humans. Wolves prefer to live in the wild, where they can hunt for their food. Clearly, dogs belong with people, while wolves do not.
options: Dogs are better suited to living with people than wolves are.
Wolves and dogs are members of the same animal family, so they have many things in common.
